methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

data.narrative:
Stratek Global operates in the nuclear energy sector at the growth stage. The comparable universe comprises 11 peers across nuclear energy: 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private firms, including Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this valuation. The nuclear energy sector contains only 8 same-sector peers overall, and filtering for stage alignment and valuation tier comparability yields fewer than the three datapoints required for robust triangulation. Specifically, zero same-stage peers and two adjacent-stage peers were identified, falling short of the minimum threshold. Reliance on an all-sector median would introduce material distortion given the specialized capital intensity and regulatory dynamics of nuclear energy.

This analysis cannot support a specific valuation range without applicable triangulation methods. The small and dispersed peer set—reflecting the frontier-stage maturity of the nuclear energy subsector—prevents quantitative synthesis at this time. Key uncertainties include stage definition boundaries within early-stage nuclear (growth vs. late-stage distinctions remain fluid), the comparability of public versus private nuclear valuations given divergent access to capital and offtake agreements, and the absence of standardized financial metrics across the peer set. A full valuation would require either expansion of the analysis to include adjacent sectors with defensible comparability, or collection of primary transaction data from recent nuclear energy funding rounds.
